programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: The victims of a bus accident all become suspects in a murder investigation, presenting Dr Sloan with a fresh mystery to solve. Starring Dick Van Dyke.(n)

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Mark suspects an eminent senator of killing his own daughter, whose behaviour was jeopardising his re-election campaign. Determined to discover the truth, he calls in Amanda to ask her advice. Dick Van Dyke and Victoria Rowell star.(n)

programme.title: CSI: Cyber
programme.desc: Following an explosion at a cinema, Avery and her team go in search of a bomber who intends to expose the truth about society's over-reliance on technology. As the investigation progresses, Ramirez and Nelson find a website which features a video of the bombing along with a sinister explanation, and they face a race to catch the culprit before the next bomb goes off. Patricia Arquette and James Van Der Beek star.(n)

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: A woman confesses to murder while under the influence of a truth serum, but Perry suspects that she is innocent and sets out to find the real culprit.(n)

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Walker has to save CD's niece after her ex-boyfriend kidnaps her and embarks on a spree of bank robberies with the girl in tow.(n)

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: A government weapons truck is hijacked as part of a plan to allow a dangerous criminal to escape from jail. Two officers are killed, but as Horatio tries to prevent further carnage and rescue a hostage, it is the members of his own team who find themselves in the line of fire. David Caruso, Emily Procter and Adam Rodriguez star.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Petty officer Jody Ray is killed by a hit-and-run driver and evidence points to staff sergeant Justin Dunne, whose guilt seems certain when DiNozzo and McGee find him washing a bloodstained knife when they go to arrest him. However, Dunne tells his lawyer Carrie Clark he was miles away in Falls Church at the time - stabbing bookmaker Donny Sullivan to death. Unable to reveal the truth to the NCIS team, Clark decides to check out the alibi herself. Crime drama, guest starring Salli Richardson-Whitfield (A Town Called Eureka).(n)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Hetty confronts Alexa Comescu and tells her Callen is dead, but the crime boss knows she is lying. Meanwhile, the agents search for their leader in Romania hoping to confirm she is alive, but when they mount a rescue attempt one of the team is shot. Real-time episode of the crime drama, starring Linda Hunt, Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J.(n)

programme.title: CSI: Cyber
programme.desc: The decomposing body of a young woman is found in the bathroom of a New York hotel, having been undisturbed for three days. When Avery Ryan's and her team investigate, they discover the victim seemed to have been ordering room service three days after her death, and her messages to staff had released vicious malware that eradicated several hours of surveillance footage from the hotel's security cameras. The chain of evidence leads Ryan to a hacker thought to have been on-site at the time of the woman's death, but as Nelson and Ramirez continue digging for clues, it becomes clear this case is far from closed. Patricia Arquette stars.(n)

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: After a house trailer is stolen, the owner calls on Perry for help as it allegedly contained a diary with the true account of a bank heist years earlier.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part one of two. A man and his son on a fishing trip hook what appears to be the body of a petty officer, but the dead man turns out to be a journalist who had been investigating the proliferation of drugs in the Navy. Meanwhile, Ziva returns to her car after a workout at the gym to find her father Eli hiding inside. Guest starring Michael Nouri.(n)

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Walker and Trivette investigate when the coach of a local football team is found dead.(n)

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Mac and Stella investigate a new fantasy game sweeping New York, and discover that harmless role-playing has begun to overlap with reality in deadly fashion. Meanwhile, a woman's death is traced to an exotic cuisine event where the dishes include live centipedes, Brazilian cockroaches, fried tarantulas and other dubious delicacies.(n)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: The CEO of a software company linked to a cyber-attack against the Department of Defence is kidnapped, and Kensi and Deeks go undercover to protect the man's family after fears over their safety. Meanwhile, Callen continues to research his past.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part two of two. After the deaths of two family members of NCIS employees, the team identifies the killer as a Swedish mercenary and the ballistics report reveals the bullets came from an Iranian arms dealer. McGee traces a wire transfer made to the hitman's ex-wife and son to an account under the name Virtue, and Ziva makes a connection after realising what the Hebrew translation is.(n)

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: Delko is rushed to hospital with potentially fatal gunshot wounds, and Horatio waits anxiously as medics try to revive him, while the rest of the team searches for his assailant. Calleigh and Ryan process the crime scene, and question a security guard who provides them with what appears to be vital information - until further evidence sheds new light on the case.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: An internal security breach forces NCIS to go into lockdown when a covert listening device is found in a pen belonging to Secretary of the Navy Sarah Porter. DiNozzo discovers that National Security Agency analyst Ellie Bishop wrote a report two years previously predicting the possibility of such an occurrence and she is brought in to assist with the investigation and track down the culprit. Guest starring Leslie Hope and Emily Wickersham.(n)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: The CEO of a software company linked to a cyber-attack against the Department of Defence is kidnapped, and Kensi and Deeks go undercover to protect the man's family after fears over their safety. Meanwhile, Callen continues to research his past.(n)

programme.title: CSI: Cyber
programme.desc: Avery Ryan's old friend Jordan Tan seeks her help in locating his missing teenage daughter Zoey. When the team looks into high-schooler's online history, they discover she had been the target of hacks, faked phone calls and social media posts, as well as a malicious website titled 'Kill Yourself Zoey Tan'. The spate of abuse bears all the hallmarks of a particularly nasty case of online bullying, but Zoey's digital footprint offers the investigators a lead in the form of online boyfriend Owen, who lives 300 miles away and seems to have invited her to visit. However, when the team ventures to Owen's home, they discover his invitation was just another facet of the hate campaign, and with Zoey's whereabout still unknown, serious concerns are raised about her wellbeing. Crime drama, starring Patricia Arquette, with guests Byron Mann and BooBoo Stewart.(n)

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: A secret marriage and missing witness keep Perry on his toes as he deals with two cases - a hit-and-run and a murder.(n)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Sam goes undercover as a bomb specialist when he investigates the death of a demolition expert, killed while driving the car of a notorious club owner. However, he faces a race against time to find explosives located across LA. Meanwhile, Callen learns more about his past and Hunter decides to reshuffle her team.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team is called to the scene of a fatal car crash involving marine lance corporal Chad Dunn and student Lyla Cutwright. Duckie notices that the woman's injuries do not match the specifics of the accident and there are no skidmarks found at the scene, leading Gibbs to declare they possibly have a murder and a suicide on their hands. Abby calls in sick for the first time after the case stirs up a traumatic incident from her childhood. Mark Harmon and Pauley Perrette star.(n)

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: The CSIs investigate the murder of a wealthy couple, and gradually begin to suspect the motive could lie in an unexpected love triangle. programme.desc: The team is called in to investigate when marine corporal Flegman is found shot to death, and a phone leads them to a hotel room where Gibbs awkwardly stumbles upon his ex-wife Diane Sterling and her FBI agent ex-husband TC Fornell. She had been attempting to call her boyfriend Eddie to break up with him, but somehow Flegman had taken possession of his phone. The team tracks down Eddie's car and discovers a clown nose along with traces of blood and he quickly becomes the prime suspect.(n)

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: Perry helps a woman accused of stealing a car and killing a truck driver. However, his inquiries lead to another dead body, making things worse.(n)

programme.desc: A corpse is discovered in a natural history museum, but the CSI detectives are unable to make an immediate identification because a swarm of flesh-eating beetles has partially devoured it. A six-year-old is later found hiding in the building, and proves to be a key witness. Meanwhile, Flack is called to a building site where a man has been poisoned with insulating foam.(n)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: The team investigates when a reporter is murdered as she prepares to break a story about a mysterious Libyan freedom fighter named El-Libi. The activist is wanted by the country's government after establishing 'radio free Libya' in the aftermath of Gaddafi's closure of the internet. Meanwhile, more details of Callen's past begin to emerge.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs and the team capture computer hacker Ajay Khan, who was recently responsible for hacking into NCIS's supposedly ultra-secure multiple-threat alert centre. They believe he could hold the key to tracking down the most-wanted cyber terrorist in the world, who may be plotting an attack using a bomb containing a strain of the Ebola virus. When Khan refuses to co-operate, Ziva and DiNozzo take him to Guantanamo Bay to be placed with people he had previously betrayed. Guest starring Vik Sahay (Chuck).(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy bargains galore.(n)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Three men stage a raid on a drug deal, taking a suitcase full of cash and killing the dealer and an undercover LAPD officer. While studying footage taken from CCTV equipment in the house, Sam recognises the distinctive hand signals of one of the suspects as being those of Navy Seal Kurt Holgate, who he served with in Bosnia. Eric studies the details of his unit and finds that a man called Jackson has been unaccounted for since the day of the attack. Drama, starring LL Cool J.(n)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: On a live-fire air force training exercise, someone hacks into the control systems of a drone and fires one of its two live missiles, striking and killing a serviceman, before piloting it away. Sam and Callen meet with a member of the National Security Agency, while Eric's inquiries lead him to a group of students on an advanced computer science course and Kensi is sent under cover to pose as its guest speaker.(n)

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: The team finds a corpse that appears to have been eaten by a large snake. When the serpent later shows up dead, the autopsy reveals drugs were being transported inside its body - and the trail leads to an old enemy of Horatio's who previously escaped justice owing to diplomatic immunity. Meanwhile, Wolfe is asked to investigate a theft, but stumbles on a case of identity fraud.(n)

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Mac and Stella investigate the fatal shooting of a mounted police officer - but the only evidence is a bullet lodged in his horse, presenting them with a dilemma. programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Amber's bad attitude and generally poor performance put her on the wrong side of Gina, who tells the flighty young PC she wants her out of the force. But Amber challenges her inspector, claiming she knows her rights and has the law on her side. Smithy is surprised to receive a call from ex-lover and gangster's wife Louise Larson, and says he wants nothing to do with her. But when he discovers she is testifying against her husband and going into witness protection, he is given plenty of food for thought. programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: An already angst-ridden Josh finds himself in even hotter water when he comes face to face with the sister of the boy he killed, while history repeats itself for Colette when her teenage daughter announces she's pregnant. Medical drama, starring Ian Bleasdale and Derek Thompson.(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Smithy tells Gina he's considering leaving the job and joining Louise in witness protection, much to his inspector's horror. Later, when he visits his former lover to talk, it's not long before they fall back into bed. Meanwhile, it's a bad day for Amber - having suffered a roasting from Gina, she now faces a pep talk from Yvonne, and ends up placing them both in jeopardy. Eventually, the penny drops for the impulsive young PC, who decides the police service isn't for her and hands in her resignation - but not before telling her colleagues exactly what she thinks of them. Mickey covers for Suzie when she makes an error of judgement, and realising she's exhausted from all her studying, he offers to help her revise.(n)

programme.title: Inspector George Gently
programme.desc: It's 1969 and six months since the shootings in Durham Cathedral, and while Gently is almost back to full fitness, Bacchus has lost his confidence and tenders his resignation. Gently is determined to help his sergeant, so he gets him involved in one last case, the death in custody of a man arrested during a street protest - an investigation that leaves both detectives questioning what it means to be a police officer at a time when attitudes to the force are changing. Detective drama, starring Alun Armstrong, James Bolam, Amanda Redman and Dennis Waterman.(n)

programme.desc: Feature-length episode. A horrific car crash poses troubling questions for Nikki when she discovers one of the victims was the recently released Anna Holland, an accomplice to murder she helped escape a life sentence four years earlier. As the investigation progresses, Nikki re-interviews key witnesses from the original case and begins to doubt her earlier conclusions. Emilia Fox stars.(n)

A fractious party of lunch guests provides the necessary pool of suspects. With guest stars John Gielgud, Geoffrey Palmer and Belinda Lang.(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: A dying man asks the team to clear the name of his son, a talented junior golfer who committed suicide after being implicated in the murder of a 10-year-old boy. Aiding the investigation is a former chief superintendent who admits she always had doubts about the teenager's guilt, but the committee members of the exclusive golf club where the victim's body was discovered prove reluctant to help the officers with their inquiries. Isla Blair and Cherie Lunghi guest star.(n)

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Nikki investigates the discovery of human bones on a former traveller site which could be the remains of a senior police officer's missing teenage daughter. However, her forensic analysis raises even greater questions and casts doubt on the guilt of the chief suspect. Meanwhile, Harry is assigned to an insurance case that takes a peculiar twist. Emilia Fox and Tom Ward star.(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. Jack Meadows' world starts to fall apart when a dying convict accuses him of a frame-up more than 20 years ago and DI Neil Manson is brought in to investigate his old boss. To make matters worse, suspicion falls on his drug-addicted son Benjamin for a series of robberies, and wife Laura blames him for getting the wayward lad locked up. As the evidence stacks up against him, Jack finds there's nowhere to turn.(n)

programme.title: London's Burning
programme.desc: Blue Watch discover that there is more to Sally than meets the eye, while Nick's suspicions of Chris grow. Elsewhere, a police chase ends in a car crash.(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home.(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home.(n)

programme.title: London's Burning
programme.desc: The group draw lots to decide who will get to have Christmas Day off duty, and Josie and Bayleaf spend the night together. Starring James Hazeldine and Katharine Rogers.(n)

programme.title: London's Burning
programme.desc: Drama series following the lives of the heroic Blue Watch at Blackwall fire station.(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. Jack Meadows' world starts to fall apart when a dying convict accuses him of a frame-up more than 20 years ago and DI Neil Manson is brought in to investigate his old boss. To make matters worse, suspicion falls on his drug-addicted son Benjamin for a series of robberies, and wife Laura blames him for getting the wayward lad locked up. As the evidence stacks up against him, Jack finds there's nowhere to turn.(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Jac is disappointed when Clifford refuses to let her participate in a pioneering remote robotic procedure, but revels in her opportunity to tell the consultant that the operation is unethical. Meanwhile, Kyla becomes nervous about whether Harvey will face charges and Lola struggles to cope with the workload in AAU.(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Nikki has her work cut out following a disaster at a building site, and wonders whether she is up to the job, while Comfort questions her beliefs after hearing a startling confession from a dying priest. Later, Jack spies a chance to get lucky with Nikki in her delicate state, but only succeeds in making the situation worse. Medical drama, starring Martina Laird, Kelly Harrison and Christine Stephen-Daly.(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. DPS officer Frank Keane warns Sam Nixon and Mickey Webb off helping Jack, but when old colleague Neil Manson catches up with the runaway copper he allows him a further 24 hours in which to clear his name. Events take a sinister turn when Vince Parker is found dead, and it's left to Sam and Mickey to spot the links between Jack's case and the beating up of son Benjamin in prison.(n)

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Gita finds she's not the only one going to the temple to pay respects to her mother. Steve's still searching for Della. Binnie makes her piece with Sharon.(n)

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Sharon catches a thief, Alan receives a worrying letter, and Pat visits Clacton to look for Frank. Meanwhile, Steve finally receives a letter from Della, but is it news he wants to hear?(n)

programme.title: London's Burning
programme.desc: Nick discovers the truth about Chris, while a fire at a cinema prompts an extraordinary act of bravery. Starring Andrew Kazamia and Jonathan Guy Lewis.(n)

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: A freak storm disrupts village life, bringing down power lines and causing a string of accidents. Jack and Beth immediately rush out to do their bit, working round the clock to treat casualties, saving strangers as well as close friends injured in the general devastation. Amanda Burton and Kevin Whately star as the medics.(n)

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Auntie Wainwright gives the boys a gas grill, but they have difficulty working out how the pressure valve works, bringing an explosive touch to their barbecue. programme.desc: The four remaining contenders face two more culinary challenges as they battle for a place in the semi-finals. programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef

programme.title: The Other One

Ellie White and Lauren Socha star, alongside Caroline Quentin.(n)

programme.title: The Secret History of My Family
programme.desc: The documentary charting social history over the centuries tells the story of an Edwardian family locked in benefit dependency, and of a mother who was accused of a shocking crime against her own child. Susan Nelson and her family live on government welfare - did her descendants follow a similar path or did they manage to move up in life? As the film reveals, more than 100 years on, some of them still rely on benefits, while others have freed their families from the grinding poverty of their ancestor.(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond.(n)

programme.title: Terry and Mason's Great Food Trip
programme.desc: Broadcaster Terry Wogan and London cabbie Mason McQueen continue their culinary quest, heading for Wells in Somerset, which is one of Britain's smallest cities. The pair visit the famous cathedral close as they begin their search for the region's most sought-after delicacies, and take in the medieval marketplace to sample cheddar cheese and sparkling cider. They also try Mendip wallfish cooked in fresh herbs and cider-flavoured chorizo, before rounding off their sojourn with a banquet fit for the abbot himself.(n)

programme.title: Wimbledon Rewind
programme.desc: Clare Balding presents a look back at Goran Ivanisevic's finals at the All England Club, against Andre Agassi in 1992 and Pat Rafter in 2001.(n)

programme.title: Len and Ainsley's Big Food Adventure
programme.desc: Len has his first taste of Middle Eastern food and samples Peruvian ceviche in central London. Later, Ainsley takes inspiration from the day and heads to the kitchen to cook chicken shawarma.(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: In Muncaster, Cumbria, hundreds of people turn up with collectibles and treasures they want to take to auction. Antiques experts James Lewis and Caroline Hawley uncover a few gems, including a dazzling decanter and an art nouveau picture frame. Presenter Paul Martin also finds time to visit Coniston Water in the Lake District.(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize.(n)

programme.title: Greatest Escapes to the Country
programme.desc: Steve Brown presents a compilation of memorable Escape to the Country mystery houses, from secret gardens to upside-down houses, and shiny new builds to rustic old thatches.(n)

programme.title: Remarkable Places to Eat
programme.desc: Leading chefs take maitre d' and First Dates star Fred Sirieix to experience dining at their favourite restaurants and explore what goes into making them so special. This time, chef Michel Roux Jr takes Fred Sirieix to some of his favourite restaurants in Paris. First, the pair visit one of the most famous restaurants in the city, La Tour D'Argent, where they experience a signature dish that has been on the menu since 1890. Moving away from restaurants that offer expensive indulgence, Michel takes Fred to a place reviving one of the simplest and cheapest French classics - the ham baguette. For the final stop of their Parisian trip, the pair visit Brasserie La Couple, which boasts the biggest dining room in Paris.(n)

programme.title: Wimbledon: The Best of the Championship
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents from the All England club, where she is joined by Tim Henman and Boris Becker in the studio and by John McEnroe and Martina Navratilova via video link to look back on highlights of the past. Viewers also have the opportunity to contribute with online votes, quizzes and the chance to send in their attempts at trick shots for review.(n)

programme.title: Marvellous
programme.desc: Toby Jones takes the lead role in this feature-length fact-based comedy drama from writer Peter Bowker (Occupation, Eric and Ernie), inspired by the life of Neil Baldwin. Although he was diagnosed as having learning difficulties, Neil has constantly confounded people's expectations during a colourful career that has seen him work as a circus clown and a kit man for Stoke City, and recently led to him being awarded an honorary degree by Keele University in recognition of his contributions to campus life. Just as Neil doesn't like to be labelled, the drama also mixes biopic, musical and fantasy elements to tell this unique story. Gemma Jones, Tony Curran and Nicholas Gleaves also star, while Gary Lineker appears as himself.(n)

programme.title: Grand Tours of Scotland
programme.desc: Paul Murton is on Barra, where he witnesses some of the great spectacles of the natural world.(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events. Presented by Emily Maitlis.(n)

programme.title: Weather

programme.title: Black Is the New Black
programme.desc: British black people who have succeeded in their chosen field discuss the challenges they have had to overcome to reach their current status and which have prevented others from doing the same, including tokenism, self-censorship, lack of capital and having to work twice as hard to prove themselves worthy. With contributions from Naomi Campbell, Trevor McDonald, Lenny Henry and Thandie Newton.(n)

programme.title: Black Is the New Black
programme.desc: The programme concludes with a look at the state of the nation for black people in Britain in the present day. The programme examines how the London Olympics and the election of Barack Obama offered hope for a brighter future where racism would eventually become a thing of the past - but also reflects on an uncertain future in the wake of more recent events. Featuring contributions from Naomi Campbell, David Harewood, Dizzee Rascal, Trevor Nelson and Trevor McDonald.(n)

programme.title: See Hear
programme.desc: Yvonne and Julian learn about Brian Duffy and Ace Mahba's experience in Seoul, how lockdown has benefited our environment and the experience of deaf people within the Black Lives Matter movement. With voiceover.(n)

programme.title: You Are What You Wear
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal hosts the fashion makeover show, with volunteers including a furniture restorer who has taken to wearing her husband's clothes, a budding Paralympian who is looking for a grown-up, wheelchair-friendly style and a hip-hop aficionado who wants to upgrade to a smarter street look. Finally, one of the stylists gets to work on a military wife and full-time mum who, after spending half her marriage living on army bases, says she has forgotten what fashion is.(n)

programme.title: Jay Blades' Home Fix
programme.desc: Aided by a rich catalogue of BBC home and garden archive, Jay offers tips on ways to tackle outstanding tasks and encourages viewers to become self-sufficient in their own homes.(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.(n)

programme.title: Greatest Escapes to the Country
programme.desc: Steve Brown presents a compilation of memorable Escape to the Country mystery houses, from secret gardens to upside-down houses, and shiny new builds to rustic old thatches.(n)

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Five dealers compete to buy items including an unusual musical instrument, a perfume bottle and a piece of medical equipment. Presented by Nigel Havers.(n)

programme.title: Keeping Britain Fed
programme.desc: Sara Cox and Ade Adepitan gain access to some of Britain's biggest supermarkets and their suppliers to see how they are dealing with the coronavirus outbreak, the most testing time in their history. Sara finds out what goes on in one supermarket over 24 hours, from opening to closing and all through the night, to see how they are coping during the crisis. Elsewhere, Ade finds out how the nations' farms, factories and depots have risen to the challenge to produce and supply our supermarkets with food in these exceptional circumstances.(n)

programme.title: Britain's Secret Seas
programme.desc: Explorer Paul Rose teams up with biologist Tooni Mahto and underwater archaeologist Frank Pope to explore the secrets of the waters surrounding Britain. The trio begin by highlighting marine life in and around the western seas, including basking sharks off the Cornish shore and spider crabs in the shallows of south Wales, as well as creatures in a protected area around Lundy Island off the Devon coast.(n)

programme.title: Terry and Mason's Great Food Trip
programme.desc: Broadcaster Terry Wogan and London cabbie Mason McQueen continue their culinary journey in Salisbury, where they taste lardy cakes in the historic market place and eat venison stew in a coaching inn with a very dubious past. They also visit the home of watercress, enjoy a curry cooked on a fire where the locals' earliest ancestors lived and meet a 12ft giant who has presided over the city since the 14th century.(n)

programme.title: Wimbledon Rewind

programme.title: Len and Ainsley's Big Food Adventure

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Interesting finds from the show's travels around the country, including a late-19th-century Steiff cow on rollers and a joystick from a World War One aeroplane.(n)

programme.desc: Nicki Chapman presents a compilation of Escape to the Country house-hunts in Dorset, including visits to thatched cottages full of possibilities for a rural West Country relocation.(n)

programme.title: Remarkable Places to Eat
programme.desc: Nisha Katona takes Fred Sirieix to San Sebastian for a mind-blowing meal at one of the world's top restaurants and to visit a tiny sports bar to learn the secrets of the perfect tortilla. The first place in Nisha's little black book is Mugaritz, ranked as the ninth best restaurant in the world and famous for wild experimentation - something that is made clear as soon as the menus arrive. Instead of a list of dishes to select, the Mugaritz menu is two pictures, one of a swimming fish, one of a flying fish, and Nisha and Fred make their choice, but have little clue what they are going to eat.(n)

programme.title: Wimbledon: The Best of the Championship
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents from the All England club, where she is joined by Tim Henman and Boris Becker in the studio and by John McEnroe and Martina Navratilova via video link to look back on highlights of the past. programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig takes the quiz in a 'ridiculous' direction this evening. Trying to come up with the answers are Alan Davies, Tom Allen, Susan Calman and Zoe Lyons.(n)

programme.title: The First Team
programme.desc: Chris Booth's methods are bringing results but that doesn't stop Petey plotting to bring an end to his run of success. Jack gets a girlfriend and plans a special party with a romantic surprise, Mattie tries to protect Chris from a shocking revelation and Benji has had enough of his social media manager and decides to fire him. Football comedy, starring Jake Short, Jack McMullen, Shaquille Ali-Yebuah and Theo Barklem-Biggs.(n)

programme.title: What We Do in the Shadows
programme.desc: Colin Robinson gets promoted at work and his new status threatens the balance of power in the vampire house. Comedy, starring Mark Proksch, Kayvan Novak and Matt Berry.(n)

programme.title: What We Do in the Shadows
programme.desc: Laszlo is panicked by the reappearance of Jim the Vampire, a vengeful enemy from the past, forcing him to flee the house and go into hiding in a bid to avoid certain death. Mark Hamill guest stars in the comedy, with Matt Berry, Kayvan Novak and Natasia Demetriou.(n)

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: Documentary examining how the National Enquirer became the most infamous tabloid in America by churning out obscene stories that blur the lines between truth and fiction.(n)

programme.title: Dragons' Den: Best Ever Pitches
programme.desc: Joe Lycett narrates a selection of memorable moments when the pitchers turned the tables on the panel, including the poker game entrepreneur who forced the dragons to compete against each other for his business. Plus, the inventor of a phone app who provoked the wrath of the panellists and a rejected contestant who went on to become a big commercial success.(n)

programme.title: A House Through Time
programme.desc: David Olusoga traces the lives of 10 Guinea Street's occupants through the Second World War to the present day, discovering stories of love, loss and renewal. In the run-up to the war, the house is occupied by the younger generation of the Wallingtons, the long-term owners of number 10, who move back to the house after several years living in the rural Somerset. The Wallingtons are letting rooms to lodgers when war is declared in 1939, and would have sheltered in their cellar as German bombs rained down on Bristol. Over the 10 months of the Bristol Blitz, the ancient city is devastated and 3,000 houses destroyed. Number 10 itself is almost among them, as a bomb falls directly on Guinea Street, only missing the house by a whisker.(n)

programme.title: Jay Blades' Home Fix
programme.desc: Joe Swift shows how to bring a lawn back to life, AJ Rose demonstrates how easy it is to put down decking, and Jay has a tip to help keep tools tidy.(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.(n)

programme.title: Greatest Escapes to the Country
programme.desc: Steve Brown presents a compilation of Escape to the Country house-hunts in Dorset, including visits to thatched cottages full of possibilities for a rural West Country relocation.(n)

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: The dealers are offered to chance to bid on an art deco cigarette case, a wooden bureau, a china sheepdog by Beswick and a Clarice Cliff preserve bowl. Presented by Nigel Havers.(n)

programme.title: Great American Railroad Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his journey to Quebec City, beginning by boarding the train from Nova Scotia to New Brunswick. Along the way he investigates the world's biggest tide at Hopewell Rocks and admires its dramatic rock formations and caves. He also appears to defy gravity on a magnetic hill in a 1965 Pontiac Bonneville, before joining the Elsipogtog First Nation to learn about quilting and traditional dress. He also goes fishing for oysters and helps quarry 150-year-old sandstone.(n)

programme.title: Britain's Secret Seas
programme.desc: The team visits Bass Rock in the Firth of Forth, home to one of the world's largest gannet colonies. Tooni Mahto heads for St Andrews Bay to learn about communication between bottlenose dolphins, and Paul Rose meets Navy divers clearing unexploded bombs dropped by aircraft during training exercises at Cape Wrath. At the Sound of Mull, Frank Pope investigates efforts to preserve fragments of history locked away in some of the 25,000 wrecks that litter the waters around the British Isles.(n)

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Terry and Mason's Great Food Trip
programme.desc: Terry Wogan and Mason McQueen continue their culinary journey around the nation, abandoning the cab for a punt along the River Cherwell in Oxford. Terry visits the city's old jail and they investigate the origins of the Oxford sausage and the famous old Oxford marmalade factory, before meeting students at the university who have formed a society to assess the scientific benefits of pizza.(n)

programme.title: Wimbledon Rewind
programme.desc: Clare Balding presents action from two memorable finals at the All England Club - Virginia Wade v Betty Stove in 1977 and Ivan Lendl v Pat Cash in 1987.(n)

programme.title: Len and Ainsley's Big Food Adventure
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott and Len Goodman visit east London, where the former Strictly Come Dancing judge returns to his childhood sampling some Jewish delicacies and has his first taste of Vietnamese food. Chef Ainsley heads to the kitchen and cooks a mouth-watering dish inspired by the day's adventures.(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At Ragley Hall, Warwickshire, antiques experts Christina Trevanion and Charlie Ross find a selection of antiques and collectibles to take to auction. Christina gets to grips with a weighty jardiniere, Charlie tests out a horse-measuring stick on its owner and presenter Paul Martin steps into the world of folk art.(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize.(n)

programme.title: Greatest Escapes to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson recalls a selection of the greatest Escape to the Country house-hunts featuring buyers hoping to earn a living from their new country home. Whether seeking a farmhouse with established holiday lets or some land with which to earn from a potential glamping enterprise, the properties show that rural businesses can help pay the bills.(n)

programme.title: Wimbledon: The Best of the Championship
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents from the All England club, where she is joined by Tim Henman and Boris Becker in the studio and by John McEnroe and Martina Navratilova via video link to look back on highlights of the past. Viewers also have the opportunity to contribute with online votes, quizzes and the chance to send in their attempts at trick shots for review. The programme also takes a look behind the scenes and into the inner sanctum of one of the most beautiful sporting venues in the world.(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don offers tips on maintaining the vegetable plot for continuous crops. From her garden in Devon, Carol Klein gives a comprehensive guide to getting plants for free, turning her attention to climbers like honeysuckle and clematis. In Brighton, cameras return to Kate Bradbury's garden to see how the once barren plot is being used by wildlife. Plus, from the archives, Rachel de Thame's visit to Gloucestershire's Kiftsgate Court Gardens, where she heard about the three generations of female gardeners who have made their mark there.(n)

programme.title: A Wild Year
programme.desc: The seasons are marked on Wales' Pembrokeshire's coast by the arrival of the puffins, which court by tapping their multi-coloured bills. Farmers grow the earliest potatoes, while sheep are employed to maintain the clifftop pastures for the rare choughs that use their long red beaks to probe for grubs. As autumn arrives, the grey seals produce their white-coated pups just before the gales mark the might of winter. Using time-lapse techniques, this documentary brings fresh insight into the restless rhythms of the countryside in an exploration of how and why this region is the way it is. Narrated by Toby Jones.(n)

programme.title: Lewis Capaldi Live
programme.desc: The breakthrough pop sensation performs the biggest headline show of his career at Croxteth Park in Liverpool, entertaining a sell-out crowd of 12,500 people. Accompanied by the 60-piece Manchester Camerata, Lewis delights the audience with hits from his platinum-selling debut album and a special surprise cover of a Beatles classic.(n)

programme.title: Borg vs McEnroe (2017)
programme.desc: During the summer of 1980, acclaimed Swedish tennis player Bjorn Borg is training for an unprecedented fifth consecutive Wimbledon men's singles title, with only one thing standing in his way - the brash and abrasive young American John McEnroe. A rivalry develops between the pair, culminating in one of the greatest matches of all time in that year's final. Biopic, starring Sverrir Gudnason, Shia LaBeouf and Stellan Skarsgard.(n)

programme.title: Books That Made Britain
programme.desc: Qasa Alom discovers how Birmingham was the inspiration for The Lord of the Rings.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross demonstrates how to paint an expansive barn, half covered in a snowy setting of frostbitten foliage and sun-kissed sky.(n)

programme.title: Genius of the Ancient World
programme.desc: Bettany Hughes' series profiling the most celebrated thinkers of the Ancient world continues as she turns her attention to Socrates. Heading to Greece, she details how the Athenian philosopher secured a reputation as an influential maverick. She also highlights how his contributions as one of the founders of Western philosophy did not please his detractors, as his outspoken defence of his beliefs ultimately led to his execution.(n)

programme.title: Ian Hislop's Stiff Upper Lip: An Emotional History of Britain
programme.desc: The broadcaster explores how the British have expressed their feelings throughout history, and considers the ways in which the nation has been shaped by its people's relationship with their emotions. He begins by looking back at 19th-century Britain, where he believes the idea of a 'stiff upper lip' first came to prominence, and explores the attitudes of figures including James Boswell and Mary Wollstonecraft to sentiment and rationality. He also contrasts the characters of Admiral Nelson and the Duke of Wellington, plays cricket on the Champs-Elysees, and reveals why the bulldog became a national symbol.(n)

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: Documentary laying bare Chechnya's deadly war against its gay citizens, which reveals the bravery of those running rescue missions to protect the republic's LGBTQ community.(n)

programme.title: Jailed in America
programme.desc: Roger Ross Williams examines the American prison system, starting in his home town of Easton, Pennsylvania, and soon finding himself navigating a Byzantine maze of police precincts, courtrooms, local jails, maximum security prisons, and corporate empires. As he begins to explore a massive and dysfunctional system, he encounters complicit politicians and prison profiteers, each with their own self-serving motivations to maintain the status quo.(n)

programme.title: Pride and Prejudice
programme.desc: Despite Miss Bingley's efforts to keep them apart, Darcy and Elizabeth's relationship grows more affectionate - until bad news arrives from Longbourn about Lydia and Wickham. Jane Austen adaptation, starring Jennifer Ehle, Colin Firth and Julia Sawalha.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross demonstrates how to paint an expansive barn, half covered in a snowy setting of frostbitten foliage and sun-kissed sky.(n)

programme.title: Books That Made Britain
programme.desc: Qasa Alom discovers how Birmingham was the inspiration for The Lord of the Rings.(n)

programme.title: Genius of the Ancient World
programme.desc: Bettany Hughes' series profiling the most celebrated thinkers of the Ancient world continues as she turns her attention to Socrates. Heading to Greece, she details how the Athenian philosopher secured a reputation as an influential maverick. She also highlights how his contributions as one of the founders of Western philosophy did not please his detractors, as his outspoken defence of his beliefs ultimately led to his execution.(n)

programme.title: Books That Made Britain
programme.desc: Simon O'Brien celebrates children's authors from north-west England, and admires the landscapes that inspired their creations.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross paints a larger-than-landscape featuring layers and layers of mountains as far as the eye can see.(n)

programme.title: Tess of the D'Urbervilles
programme.desc: Angel and Tess are on their honeymoon when he decides it is time for them to admit their former transgressions, but while she forgives his, he is appalled by what his new wife tells him and heads off to Brazil to set up a farm there by himself. She then finds a job on a bleak farm run by Groby and bumps into Alec, who claims he is a changed man and proposes marriage. Gemma Arterton stars.(n)

programme.title: Britain's Forgotten Slave Owners
programme.desc: First of a two-part documentary. Historian David Olusoga explores the archive records of Britain's 46,000 slave owners and discovers a surprising range of people who owned slaves, including clergymen and shopkeepers. The compensation records reveal how the government of the day paid £17billion in today's money to slave owners following abolition of slavery in 1834, while the 800,000 registered slaves received nothing. David also visits Barbados and traces how Britain's slave economy emerged in the 17th century, and in Jamaica he reads from the diaries of a notorious slave owner.(n)

programme.title: Britain's Forgotten Slave Owners
programme.desc: Part two of two. David Olusoga traces the bitter propaganda war waged between the pro-slavery lobby and the abolitionists and reveals how, in 1834, the government arrived at the decision to compensate Britain's 46,000 slave owners with the equivalent of £17billion in today's money. He goes on to investigate what happened to the wealth generated from the slave trade and the compensation pay-out, revealing links to aspects of Britain's industrialisation in the 19th century, expansion of the railway network, and a number of the country's most well-known institutions.(n)

programme.title: Whoever Heard of a Black Artist? Britain's Hidden Art History
programme.desc: Brenda Emmanus follows Sonia Boyce as she prepares an exhibition at Manchester Art Gallery highlighting black and Asian artists who helped shape the history of British art. Featuring interviews with ground-breaking artists from the Windrush generation, 1960s counterculture revolutionaries and members of the Black Art Movement of the 1980s, with contributors including Rasheed Araeen, Lubaina Himid, Yinka Shonibare and Althea McNish.(n)

programme.title: Art of Persia
programme.desc: Samira Ahmed goes back to when Persia faced its gravest threat - Genghis Khan - and reveals how the Safavids, who succeeded the Mongols, give Iran a powerful new identity.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross paints a larger-than-landscape featuring layers and layers of mountains as far as the eye can see.(n)

programme.title: Books That Made Britain
programme.desc: Simon O'Brien celebrates children's authors from north-west England, and admires the landscapes that inspired their creations.(n)

programme.title: What Do Artists Do All Day?
programme.desc: The daily life of children's book illustrator Shirley Hughes, who chats about her love of drawing and the challenges of coming up with new ideas.(n)

programme.title: Ian Hislop's Stiff Upper Lip: An Emotional History of Britain
programme.desc: The broadcaster explores how the British have expressed their feelings throughout history, and considers the ways in which the nation has been shaped by its people's relationship with their emotions. He begins by looking back at 19th-century Britain, where he believes the idea of a 'stiff upper lip' first came to prominence, and explores the attitudes of figures including James Boswell and Mary Wollstonecraft to sentiment and rationality. He also contrasts the characters of Admiral Nelson and the Duke of Wellington, plays cricket on the Champs-Elysees, and reveals why the bulldog became a national symbol.(n)

programme.title: Opera Italia
programme.desc: Antonio Pappano explores the career of Puccini, visiting his former home near Lucca, the conservatory in Milan where he studied, and locations from his operas, including the Palazzo Farnese and Castel Sant'Angelo in Rome. He also takes a closer look at the composer's original manuscripts, and talks to singers and directors from productions of his best-known works.(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Simon Mayo presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on16 November 1989 and featuring performances by 808 State, Lisa Stansfield, Inner City, UB40, Kaoma, Iron Maiden and Phil Collins.(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Jackie Brambles and Jenny Powell present the edition first broadcast on 23 November 1989, featuring performances by Big Fun, Fine Young Cannibals,, the Stone Roses, Bobby Brown, Jeff Wayne and Happy Mondays with Kirsty MacColl.(n)

programme.title: TOTP2
programme.desc: A selection of memorable Top of the Pops performances from some of the biggest names in Latin music, including Ricky Martin, Jennifer Lopez, Shakira, Santana, Gloria Estefan and Lou Bega.(n)

programme.title: Huey Morgan's Latin Music Adventure
programme.desc: The 6Music presenter embarks on a quest to understand Latin Music's enduring appeal. Arriving in Rio, Huey learns that Brazil's newly elected government has come out against the hedonistic atmosphere of carnival and that Mangueira Samba School group is just one of many planning a protest. Later, he meets Gilberto Gil, who pioneered a new, politically conscious sound known as Tropicália. It was so radical - and its social implications so profound - that Gil was imprisoned and finally exiled in 1969. In Salvador, Huey meets percussionist Carlinhos Brown to talk about how afro positive music has shaped the sound of Brazil.(n)

programme.title: Latin Music: A Session With
programme.desc: Bossacucanova is a Brazilian musical group combining traditional Bossa Nova with electronica. Pioneer of bossa nova Roberto Menescal, joins the band - which co-comprises his son Márcio - in their Rio studio, for a re-interpreting of classic Bossa hits. With contributions by Mustafa Baba Aussa and Marcio Menescal. Partly in Portuguese with English subtitles.(n)

programme.title: Marc Bolan: Cosmic Dancer
programme.desc: Intimate biography, narrated in the pop singer's own words, first shown to mark the 70th anniversary of his birth and the 40th of his death. With the dawn of the 1970s and the break-up of the Beatles, Bolan became the gender-bending glam rocker whose band T. Rex revitalised the British music scene. However, this film - incorporating hitherto unseen home movies shot by record producer Tony Visconti and Bolan himself - reveals a far more complex and driven figure whose life was cut short at the age of 29. Contributors include the singer's partner, Gloria Jones.(n)

programme.title: So Awkward
programme.desc: When Lily is upset to realise Matt is going out with cool girl Greta Masters, Jas plans a make-over slumber party to cheer her up. Unfortunately it clashes with a rare comet sighting, which Martha and Ollie are desperate to experience. They have even been allowed to borrow the very precious school telescope for the evening. Ollie is smuggled in to Lily's house by Martha to watch secretly from the attic, with Martha planning to split her time between both events. After much chaos and confusion, and with the presence of Ollie in the house rumbled, a compromise is finally reached, where the girls all get to do both the make-over and watch the rare comet. At the end of the make-over session Martha and Jas are horrified to see Lily has transformed herself to look exactly like Greta in a bid to impress Matt. To spare Lily's feelings they pretend to like her new look, but are relieved the next day in school to see Lily returned to her normal, natural self. Lily has realised it's better to be.(n)

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef

programme.title: The Other One
programme.desc: The continuation of Holly Walsh and Pippa Brown's character-driven comedy about two half-sisters, both called Catherine Walcott, who until the aftermath of the death of their father had no idea that other existed. programme.title: Alan Bennett's Talking Heads
programme.desc: Kristin Scott Thomas stars in this remake of The Hand of God, which originally starred Eileen Atkins. Celia runs an antiques shop and considers herself quite an expert in her field. After she buys a lovely old frame containing the smudgy drawing of a finger, she discovers she is not quite as knowledgeable as she thought.(n)

programme.title: You Are What You Wear
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal hosts the fashion makeover show, with volunteers including the husband-and-wife slimmer of the year champions, who are looking for outfits suitable for a glitzy, gold-themed ball, and 24-year-old Bournemouth woman Grace, who would like a wardrobe suitable for her new high-flying job. The stylists also get to choose over Jill, a 56-year-old raver who is looking for a style she can throw some serious shapes in, and Mel from Doncaster, who is keen to swap her all-weather obsession with flip flops and leggings for a sophisticated new look.(n)

programme.title: Ambulance - Liverpool
programme.desc: Advanced paramedic Kirsten is faced with an unusual job. A call has come in from police about a burglar who has injured himself trying to escape from the house he was looting. As she arrives, she finds him in agony with a nasty open fracture on his leg. Later, Kelsey and Dave are called to a pregnant woman in a lot of pain who worries she may be in early labour, and Gareth and Gabby treat a woman who is struggling with her breathing. But then two high-priority calls come in - both of which involve children.(n)

programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers visit Keely and Bruce in Hucknall, Nottingham, to create a garden that successfully integrates a hot tub into the finished design.(n)

programme.title: Alan Bennett's Talking Heads
programme.desc: Kristin Scott Thomas stars in this remake of The Hand of God, which originally starred Eileen Atkins. Celia runs an antiques shop and considers herself quite an expert in her field. After she buys a lovely old frame containing the smudgy drawing of a finger, she discovers she is not quite as knowledgeable as she thought.(n)

programme.title: You Are What You Wear
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal hosts the fashion makeover show, with volunteers including the husband-and-wife slimmer of the year champions, who are looking for outfits suitable for a glitzy, gold-themed ball, and 24-year-old Bournemouth woman Grace, who would like a wardrobe suitable for her new high-flying job. The stylists also get to choose over Jill, a 56-year-old raver who is looking for a style she can throw some serious shapes in, and Mel from Doncaster, who is keen to swap her all-weather obsession with flip flops and leggings for a sophisticated new look.(n)

programme.title: Ambulance - Liverpool
programme.desc: Advanced paramedic Kirsten is faced with an unusual job. A call has come in from police about a burglar who has injured himself trying to escape from the house he was looting. As she arrives, she finds him in agony with a nasty open fracture on his leg. Later, Kelsey and Dave are called to a pregnant woman in a lot of pain who worries she may be in early labour, and Gareth and Gabby treat a woman who is struggling with her breathing. But then two high-priority calls come in - both of which involve children.(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts explores an end terrace in Mountain Ash, Rhondda Cynon Taff, Martel Maxwell visits a bungalow in Gillingham, Kent, and Dion Dublin checks out a derelict property in Walsall in the West Midlands.(n)

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood introduces footage of a jeweller who refused to budge even though he was looking down the barrel of a gun, and two thieves who stole a tortoise from a zoo.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Margherita Taylor is property hunting in Shropshire with a couple of keen gardeners who want their £425,000 to buy them a country home with ample space for them and their hot tub. While in the county, Margherita samples some of Shropshire's pioneering local produce at Chadwell Park Farm, which has successfully turned its hand to making vegetable juices from its salad crops.(n)

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: The four remaining contenders face two more culinary challenges as they battle for a place in the semi-finals. First they are split into two groups for a test of teamwork and communication. One duo has to master John Torode's dim sum selection, and the other his spiced duck noodle broth. Then, the celebrities' teamwork skills are pushed to the extreme as they are faced with their first Mass Catering Challenge, cooking lunch for over 100 staff at Acton Works - the central hub for repairs and upgrades to London Underground trains.(n)

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: The battle for the first two semi-final places in this year's competition reaches its climax, as the four remaining celebrities are tasked with cooking a faultless two-course meal that will not only be judged by John Torode and Gregg Wallace, but also by three previous contenders - Olympic long-jumper Greg Rutherford, and TV personalities Vicky Pattison and Dom Parker. All three know what it takes to get through this competition and are in for a treat as their successors pull out all the stops to try to secure their semi-final place. For those who don't cut the mustard, the journey ends here.(n)

programme.title: The People's News
programme.desc: People across Northern Ireland react to the news of the week and give their views on how their lives are being impacted by what is happening across the country. Contributors include those working on the frontline - be they nurses or doctors, drivers or food suppliers.(n)

programme.title: Keepin 'er Country at Home
programme.desc: Jim Devine welcomes viewers to his Tyrone farmhouse for a gig. Between songs, Jim reflects on his career in country music and also is joined by a special guest star for a duet.(n)

programme.title: The Other One
programme.desc: The continuation of Holly Walsh and Pippa Brown's character-driven comedy about two half-sisters, both called Catherine Walcott, who until the aftermath of the death of their father had no idea that other existed. Cathy's mother refuses to attend her hen do, and with Auntie Dawn, Marilyn, Cat and old school friend Claire on the invitation list, the budding bride begins to fear it may not be the classy, edifying affair she has anticipated. Ellie White and Lauren Socha star, alongside Caroline Quentin.(n)

programme.title: Peter Kay's Car Share
programme.desc: Dressed as Harry Potter and Hagrid respectively, John and Kayleigh are full of high spirits as they head off on their annual works' do. However they have an unwanted guest on the way home - inebriated Elsie from the deli counter who 'is saying nothing'. Comedy, co-written by and starring Peter Kay and Sian Gibson.(n)

programme.title: 3:10 to Yuma (2007)
programme.desc: A rancher seizes the chance to settle his debts and earn his son's respect by agreeing to escort a notorious outlaw to the town of Contention, where a train will take the prisoner to jail. But the journey takes a dangerous turn when the criminal tries to manipulate the rancher into letting him go - while his henchmen lie in wait. Western remake, starring Russell Crowe, Christian Bale and Gretchen Mol.(n)

programme.desc: Linda Macleod is joined by Hannah and Matthew to make sticky noodles with chicken and honey. They also learn more about where honey comes from.(n)

programme.title: Peataichean/Pets
programme.desc: Eilidh Cormack meets Callum and his pet dogs, Penny and Poppy.(n)

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Pando and Bing are playing outside in the cold, but Flop has made them hot chocolate.(n)

programme.desc: The Floogals learn how mirrors work and how they produce a reflection.(n)

programme.desc: Iain MacRae reads Taigh sa Choille, written and illustrated by Inga Moore and translated into Gaelic by Jo MacDonald.(n)

programme.title: Buntàta Amh (Big Tatties)
programme.desc: The potato goes for a walk with his friend Sliseag.(n)

programme.title: Saidheans Sporsail (Backyard Science)
programme.desc: Fun science experiments, including how to make silver shine without polishing it, and whether eyes do really lie about size. Presented by Kerry Anne MacLeod and Allan MacDonald.(n)

programme.title: Aithne air Ainmhidhean (All About Animals)
programme.desc: The life of a hyrax, creatures that share an ancestor with elephants. Cameras observe a young hyrax as he outwits eagles with his built in sunglasses, scrambles up vertical rock faces using his suction-feet and finally, after leaving his family, fights and wins a colony of his own.(n)

programme.title: Sugh an Eorna (History of Whisky)
programme.desc: John Carmichael continues the six-part series celebrating the history of Scotch whisky through the centuries.(n)

programme.desc: Anna Macleod is in conversation with guests Rhona Dougall and Aonghas MacNeacail, who recommend two books of special interest to them.(n)

programme.title: Opry an Iúir
programme.desc: Daniel O'Donnell presents a tribute to his sister Margo's musical career from the Millennium Forum in Derry, including her time as a member of the Keynotes.(n)

programme.title: Bannan (The Ties That Bind)
programme.desc: Màiri is back to square one until she speaks to Peigi, who puts a new suspect in the frame - which rocks Màiri's world. Iain is sick of Anndra's games and is forced to tell Ciorstaidh the truth. Tormod and Raonaid regret the night before, and Donna and Finlay return from holiday to find their money problems have only just begun.(n)

programme.title: Port
programme.desc: Julie Fowlis and Muireann NicAmhlaoibh visit Donegal to discover more about traditional music in the area. Accompanying them are renowned singer Margaret Stewart and fiddler Coll MacDonald. They discover that Donegal is a hotbed of talent and perform with acclaimed singer Rita Gallagher, musicians Dermot Byrne and Brid Harper and the Campbell brothers amongst others.(n)

programme.title: Mathair
programme.desc: Historians and her relatives tell the story of Maud Gonne, recalling her time as a muse to the poet WB Yeats, and her work for the disenfranchised and for the rights of prisoners.(n)

programme.title: Port
programme.desc: Music recorded in Edinburgh, Lewis and Belfast in 2015 and 2016, featuring the likes of Dónal Clancy, Alasdair White, Seán Mac Corraidh and Paul Meehan.(n)

programme.title: Eadar Chluich
programme.desc: Short drama adaptation of Joan Mackenzie's song Girl of the Branches, with Kate Dickie and Alison Peebles.(n)

programme.desc: Tom arrives in time to help Twigs collect bark for his 'bark-book' - taking to the skies with the Kangaroo Spell to catch all that they need for his collection. However, their hard work is in vain when their collection is stolen.(n)

programme.title: Abadas
programme.desc: The animals are delighted to hear today's word is a musical one, as they love to sing and dance.(n)

programme.title: Stòiridh
programme.desc: Donna Morrison reads An Dràgon a bha na Phlàigh by Julie Sykes, illustrated by Melanie Williamson and translated into Gaelic by Jo MacDonald.(n)

programme.title: Saidheans Sporsail (Backyard Science)
programme.desc: Fun science experiments, including how to make a set of pan pipes and smarten up a pavement with a picturesque paint job. Presented by Kerry Anne MacLeod and Allan MacDonald.(n)

programme.title: Aithne air Ainmhidhean (All About Animals)
programme.desc: The story of a grey seal preparing for life in the North Atlantic. On a cold, wet and windy beach, Sully must learn all he needs to know in just four short weeks before heading out to sea alone on the journey of a lifetime.(n)

programme.title: An Lot (The Croft)
programme.desc: Sweeny reflects on his year of full-time crofting and assesses what he has learned from the experience. Is he any nearer his dream? What have been the successes and what failures have there been? What would he do differently now looking back, and what does the future now hold?(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot.(n)

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Raine, Scott, Graham and Kelly pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught.(n)

programme.title: Bradley Walsh & Son: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: A special series looking back at Bradley and Barney's previous American adventures. Following a rocky start in Red River Gorge, Barney has another unusual activity in store with a trip to a haunted school. Later, the pair visit Louisville, the home of Muhammad Ali, where they follow in his footsteps by sparring at a local boxing ring. Travelling into Ohio, Brad and Barney head straight for Dayton where Barney flies a plane, before the pair attend a local rodeo later that evening, and Bradley has a nasty fall which lands him in hospital.(n)

programme.title: Benidorm
programme.desc: Geoff stages an intervention when Pauline falls off the wagon, but not before she has drunkenly befriended young Jodie - who was left under the not-so-watchful eye of Eddie while Billy and Sheron went for breakfast. The Solana wristbands are replaced by a new ID card system, but the technicalities of the new process prove too complicated for Mateo and the staff, Joyce goes on a date with her estate agent Monty, while Rob makes the mistake of trying to teach Joey and Tiger how to play chess.(n)

programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents.(n)

programme.title: Peston
programme.desc: Political magazine show presented by Robert Peston, featuring major interviews with MPs, topical guests and cultural figures. Including updates from social media taking in perspectives from viewers, experts and key players throughout the programme.(n)

programme.title: Wales in the Euros - The History Boys
programme.desc: A look back at Wales' historic success in the Euro 2016 tournament, featuring the most memorable moments on and off the field with players and fans. Chris Coleman's men exceeded all expectations, reaching the semi-finals of the tournament before being defeated by eventual champions Portugal. They finished top of Group B - despite losing 2-1 to England - following wins over Slovakia and Russia, and got past Northern Ireland in the Round of 16. In the quarter-finals, they beat strong favourites Belgium 3-1, but could not overcome Cristiano Ronaldo's Portugal side in the last four.(n)

programme.title: Ross Kemp Living With
programme.desc: The film-maker investigates how the housing crisis has resulted in some vulnerable families agreeing to be moved by local councils - sometimes many miles away from loved ones - just to keep a roof over their heads. He meets a mother of three whose temporary accommodation across London has ended up being her home for three years so far, and also hears from families in Bradford who say that refusing an offer of accommodation 200 miles away would bring shocking repercussions.(n)

programme.title: Britain's Brightest Celebrity Family
programme.desc: The next two heat-winning families compete in the second semi-final of the knockout tournament, bringing them one step closer to the chance to win £25,000 for their chosen charity and the coveted title of Britain's Brightest Celebrity Family. The Chase's Anne Hegerty, aka The Governess, asks the questions.(n)

programme.desc: The financial journalist presents more advice for people who have found themselves living with financial uncertainty. Martin answers viewers' questions on how the economic crisis affects family finances, jobs and consumer rights.(n)

programme.title: Billion Pound Cruises: All at Sea
programme.desc: A report on how the coronavirus pandemic brought the £120billion cruise industry to its knees and 100s of ships around the world to a standstill. The documentary looks at the role of cruise ships in spreading the virus and ask if passengers' well-being was put first, as it tells the story of the six extraordinary weeks when thousands of passengers ended up in quarantine.(n)

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: Crime drama starring Brenda Blethyn. The detective investigates a fire that rips through a holiday park on the Northumberland coast, destroying three cabins. A young woman's body is discovered and the resort's owner, Jim Viner, fears the victim is his sister Deena when he returns from a weekend away with his daughter Clare. With Jim's suspicions confirmed, a police report then reveals Clare's boyfriend Ryan and his friends had been partying near the scene hours before the blaze started, but where are they now, and where is Deena's best friend Sally? Co-starring Kenny Doughty, Cush Jumbo and Jon Morrison.(n)

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Warwick Davis in which a team of five friends from Herefordshire answers questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then tries to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money.(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot.(n)

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught.(n)

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: Crime drama starring Brenda Blethyn. The detective investigates a fire that rips through a holiday park on the Northumberland coast, destroying three cabins. A young woman's body is discovered and the resort's owner, Jim Viner, fears the victim is his sister Deena when he returns from a weekend away with his daughter Clare. With Jim's suspicions confirmed, a police report then reveals Clare's boyfriend Ryan and his friends had been partying near the scene hours before the blaze started, but where are they now, and where is Deena's best friend Sally? Co-starring Kenny Doughty, Cush Jumbo and Jon Morrison.(n)

programme.title: ITV News at Ten

programme.title: European Cup Winners: Liverpool
programme.desc: A look at Liverpool's four European Cup triumphs between 1977 and 1984, charting how manager Bob Paisley helped them to become a European superpower after his predecessor Bill Shankly had laid the foundations. With highlights of the finals and interviews with some of the key individuals involved.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Abandoned by his wife and faced with financial ruin, local farmer Richard Harvey finally reaches the end of his tether, and heads into Aidensfield to air his grievances in a particularly memorable way. Meanwhile, the arrival of a handsome stranger sets Gina's heart racing - but he could be a dangerous criminal. Vernon and David branch out into a new line of business, and soon manage to pep up a few palates with Aunt Beth's tangy horseradish sauce.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Sean tries to talk to Eric about a pay rise, Betty is busy planning Linda and Biff's wedding, and Dave offers Jan some of the money Kim gave him, but she refuses to touch it.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zoe is late for work, Terry asks Viv if she is still going to meet him in a hotel, and Jan wants to celebrate her silver wedding anniversary, but Ned is worried about the cost.(n)

programme.desc: Denise begs Ken to forgive her and tells him she does not want to leave him. Deirdre feels sorry for Tricia in prison, and Jamie asks Deirdre to put him up.(n)

programme.desc: A woman's body is discovered in Captain Hastings' garage on Bonfire Night. It transpires that the deceased was due to marry an MP - and although the evidence suggests suicide, the Belgian detective suspects foul play. David Suchet, Hugh Fraser and Philip Jackson star.(n)

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Rosamund comes up with a plan to help Edith out of her predicament, but will Violet see straight through it? Rose is frustrated when things don't go her way and Alfred realises too late he may have made a mistake. Anna is concerned by unexpected news just as she and Bates seem to be getting over the worst, Isobel finds herself the object of someone's attention and Mary asks Gillingham for a favour.(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Religious fanatic Steven Mullan is found beaten and drowned in his bath. Lewis and Hathaway discover he had been imprisoned for trying to kill well-known atheist Tom Rattan and had only recently been released. The inspector soon has reason to believe the Rattan family is hiding something from him - but nothing prepares him for the evidence Dr Hobson uncovers at the post-mortem. Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox star, with guest appearances by Jenny Seagrove and Julian Wadham.(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: The last-ever episode. When his colleague Hilda Pierce is targeted by an assassin outside MI5, Foyle must re-examine her top-secret role during the war. He suspects the shooting could be connected to the hunt for a traitor within the Special Operations Executive who may have been behind the deaths of nine agents in France. Meanwhile, Sam is faced with a dilemma when Adam and Glenvil crack down on the black market in East Peckham. Guest stars include Conleth Hill (Game of Thrones' Lord Varys).(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: The discovery of a woman's body in a car park presents Jack Frost with another challenge to his deductive powers. Delving into the victim's electronic organiser, he learns she was connected with a local religious community - and realises someone in the congregation is more interested in the pleasures of this world than the rewards of the next. David Jason stars, with Bruce Alexander and John Lyons.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian sleuth takes a break on the island of Rhodes, where an English beauty draws attention by flirting with fellow guests at the Palace Hotel. When she is found murdered, her husband is immediately suspected, but Poirot quickly deduces there is more to the case than a simple crime of passion. Whodunit, starring David Suchet.(n)

programme.desc: A series of scandals makes for an interesting few days in the quiet village of St Mary Mead, and most seem to revolve around a blustering local colonel, whose brusque manner and short temper make him unpopular with his fellow villagers. When he is found dead with a bullet through the head, Miss Marple and the reluctant DI Slack investigate. Whodunit, starring Geraldine McEwan as Miss Marple, with Jane Asher, Jason Flemying, Derek Jacobi and Miriam Margolyes.(n)

programme.desc: English country squire Marcus Waverly receives a series of anonymous letters threatening to kidnap his son Johnnie, but the case is dismissed by Scotland Yard - prompting Poirot to open his own investigation. Drama, starring David Suchet and Geoffrey Bateman.(n)

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: It's summer and as part of Rose's debutante 'coming out' she is to be presented at Buckingham Palace. The family is in London, preparing Grantham House for the busy social programme, and Martha Levinson, not one to miss such a grand occasion, arrives from New York with Cora's recently disgraced brother Harold (Sideways star Paul Giamatti). Both outspoken and larger than life, they make quite an impression on certain members of high society. Meanwhile, when the Crawleys are implicated in a scandal that threatens to engulf the monarchy, Robert goes to great lengths to protect the royal family - and his own. Costume drama, starring Lily James, Shirley MacLaine, Hugh Bonneville, Maggie Smith, Laura Carmichael and Elizabeth McGovern.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When wealthy farmer Martin Strickland is discovered bound to a tree, doused in truffle oil and mauled to death by a wild boar, the investigation leads DCI Barnaby to tyrannical celebrity chef Ruth Cameron at the upmarket Wyvern House restaurant. However, secrets from the past surface after the murderer strikes again, and the repercussions are felt further than they were originally intended. With Neil Dudgeon, Neil McCaul, Sharon Small, Marc Elliott and Arabella Weir.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The dapper Belgian sleuth goes on an Egyptian cruise on board an old steamer, but when an American heiress is murdered, his little grey cells go into overdrive. On questioning each of his fellow passengers, he realises many of them have secrets they want to remain hidden. David Suchet stars, with James Fox, Judy Parfitt, Emily Blunt, Daisy Donovan, Frances de la Tour and David Soul.(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: A student is attacked on campus, and Jack takes time off from questioning two robbery suspects to catch the thug responsible before he can strike again. The trail leads him to Boxborough - where a confrontation with a belligerent solicitor provides the clue he needs to crack the case. Starring David Jason, Bruce Alexander, John Lyons, Charlotte Bellamy and Tazmin Malleson.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Captain Hastings and the Belgian supersleuth investigate a baffling gangland murder which draws them both into the underworld of London's Little Italy. Miss Lemon turns out to have a link with the victim's valet. Whodunit, starring David Suchet and Hugh Fraser.(n)

programme.desc: Maureen decides not to tell Reg about the burglary. Alma is charmed by Stephen and thinks he is gorgeous. Kevin urges Ken to find someone to look after Daniel long term.(n)

programme.desc: Robin and his brother lock horns over Chrissy's affections, while Mr Roper tries his hand at chimney sweeping. Comedy, with Richard O'Sullivan.(n)
