programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: High Court enforcement officers Paul and Steve's first job is an eviction with a twist when they find out the landlord is the tenant's son, while their next case is also tricky and involves family members with a question mark over their immigration status who are now facing homelessness. Their final case involves a man with a history of mental illness who has reached the end of his tenancy but refuses to leave, and Steve tries to find a solution after a church minister and his mother fail to placate him.(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Documentary series giving an insight into the world of debt collection. Kevin Stokes and Brian O'Shaughnessy try to collect a debt from a technology company, but with the director nowhere to be seen, the office manager does everything she can to prove the business is no longer trading. Stewart McCracken and Elmor Victor face a heartbreaking house repossession in Merseyside, where two landlords are forced to evict a tenant recovering from a brain tumour which has cost him his job and income.(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Kevin Stokes and Brian O'Shaughnessy get into a doorstep confrontation with a debtor who wants to prove her family business is bankrupt. However, when tempers flare Brian is assaulted and the agents call the police. Elsewhere, Paul Bohill and Steve Pinner face evicting people fromthree properties owned by the same landlord in north London, and Brian and Delroy Anglin collect £2,000 debt owed to a vet in Kent.(n)

programme.title: Neighbours

programme.title: Police Interceptors

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Briscoe and Green investigate the murder of a white police officer, and the trail ultimately leads to a former black rights activist. As the trial gets under way, racial tensions in the city are inflamed with the emergence of evidence linking the dead man to several white supremacist organisations. McCoy realises whichever verdict is returned, it is guaranteed to be greeted with public outrage.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Van Buren orders Briscoe and Green to reinvestigate unsolved cases, starting with the murder of a teenager 20 years previously. However, they discover much of the evidence gathered at the time has vanished, and the detective in charge of the case may have pursued an innocent man to protect the real culprit. An unexpected confession allows the case to end up in court again, but legal trickery makes a conviction an unlikely prospect.(n)

programme.desc: Jerry Orbach's final appearance in the crime drama. programme.title: Law & Order

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Briscoe and Green investigate the case of a couple found dead in their apartment, following a trail of drugs and money to the wife of an army officer responsible for anti-narcotics operations in Colombia. programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team is called in to investigate when marine corporal Flegman is found shot to death, and a phone leads them to a hotel room where Gibbs awkwardly stumbles upon his ex-wife Diane Sterling and her FBI agent ex-husband TC Fornell. She had been attempting to call her boyfriend Eddie to break up with him, but somehow Flegman had taken possession of his phone. programme.title: NCIS

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: For hundreds of years, psychiatry has treated voices and hallucinations as an enemy. But today new scientific and psychological insights into how the brain works are leading to a radical rethink on what such experiences are - and how they should be treated. Cameras follow three people living with voices, hallucinations and paranoia, to explore what causes this kind of phenomena.(n)

programme.title: The Dark Man (1951)
programme.desc: A detective investigates a series of murders, while the mysterious killer pursues an actress who may be able to identify him. Thriller, starring Edward Underdown and Maxwell Reed.(n)

programme.desc: A devoted wife journeys to Scotland in a bid to prove her husband has been wrongly accused of murdering his former girlfriend. Thriller, with Greta Gynt, Hugh Williams, Marius Goring and Francis L Sullivan.(n)

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Exploring the districts of Woolwich, Plumstead and Deptford.(n)

programme.title: Underworld at War

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: For hundreds of years, psychiatry has treated voices and hallucinations as an enemy. programme.title: Diana: Last Days of a Princess
programme.desc: Genevieve O'Reilly and Patrick Baladi star as Diana, Princess of Wales and Dodi Al Fayed in a docu-drama charting the couple's last days, culminating in the fatal crash in the Alma Tunnel, Paris. Including interviews with Mohamed Al Fayed, bodyguard Kez Wingfield and masseuse Myriah Daniels.(n)

programme.title: Upstairs Downstairs
programme.desc: It is January 1936, and diplomat Sir Hallam and Lady Agnes Holland (Ed Stoppard and Keeley Hawes) move into 165 Eaton Place - closely followed by his widowed mother and her wayward sister. But they also need a full serving staff, for which Lady Agnes turns to an agency run by none other than Rose Buck (Jean Marsh), who used to work at the house. Eileen Atkins, Art Malik, Anne Reid and Adrian Scarborough co-star.(n)

programme.title: Chubby Funny (2016)
programme.desc: An aspiring actor continually blames others for his failure to become a big star. He decides to give himself a year living in London to finally land his big break - but the personal crises that have dogged him all his life are not going away. Comedy drama, directed by and starring Harry Michell, with Augustus Prew and Jeff Rawle.(n)

programme.title: Hot Property (2016)
programme.desc: A corporate spy loses her job after getting caught embezzling, and is no longer able to fund her lavish lifestyle. Faced with being evicted from her rented home, she puts all her skills of deception into fending off the landlord, replacement tenants and a psychotic estate agent. Comedy, starring MyAnna Buring and Tom Rhys Harries.(n)

programme.title: Kevcoin: The Movie (2018)
programme.desc: Wannabe music producer Kevin Powder hatches a scheme to get rich by creating his own cryptocurrency, with each token valued at a trillion dollars. But to make his plan a reality, he needs to hit the streets in search of investors. Spoof documentary, starring Jason Attar and Dominic Geraghty.(n)

programme.title: 3 Steps to Heaven (1995)
programme.desc: A man's body is found in the Thames, and his girlfriend is convinced he was murdered. Adopting a series of disguises, she sets out to find the last three men to see him alive, and discover the truth about what happened - but is unprepared for the secrets she uncovers about the man she thought she knew. Drama, starring Katrin Cartlidge, Frances Barber and James Fleet.(n)

programme.title: Bloomfield (1970)
programme.desc: An Israeli footballer nearing the end of his career is tempted to accept a bribe to throw his final game. A devoted young fan and a woman who is in love with him prick his conscience, but even if he chooses to do the right thing, his failing skills may not be enough to triumph over younger, fitter opposition. Sporting drama based on Joseph Gross' novel, co-directed by and starring Richard Harris, with Romy Schneider and Kim Burfield.(n)

programme.title: Very Important Person (1961)
programme.desc: A pompous Allied scientist ends up in a PoW camp after crash-landing during a reconnaissance mission over Germany. His uncompromising attitude and a series of misunderstandings initially earn him the enmity of his fellow inmates, but his popularity improves when he devises an audacious escape plan. Second World War comedy, with James Robertson Justice, Stanley Baxter, Leslie Phillips and Eric Sykes.(n)

programme.title: Underworld at War
programme.desc: Peter Williams investigates the ration-book fraudsters and coupon fiddlers spawned by the restrictions of the Second World War, and looks at the career of gangster Frankie Fraser, for whom the war provided an opportunity to get rich quick.(n)

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: Dallas Campbell examines films from the Horizon archive to discover what science can teach people about dogs. He investigates the origins of domestic breeds and learns about the role humans played in shaping canine evolution, before asking why the bond between mankind and dogs is particularly strong - and whether it can be improved in the future.(n)

programme.title: The Moodys
programme.desc: Kevin and Maree's wedding anniversary prompts the family to gather for a celebration at the local veterans' centre. As the clan spends time together to remember how important family is, an unforeseen occurrence at the party triggers a series of catastrophes.(n)

programme.title: Content Content
programme.desc: Sitcom based in the offices of Content Content, a digital content agency that nobody, including the employees, can pronounce correctly.(n)

programme.title: Away (2016)
programme.desc: In Blackpool, an unlikely friendship blossoms between a lonely, suicidal widower and a young woman trying to escape an abusive ex-boyfriend. Drama, starring Timothy Spall, Juno Temple and Matt Ryan.(n)

programme.title: One Night in Powder (2015)
programme.desc: A clueless music promoter hits the streets of East London, hoping to put together the greatest club night the capital has ever seen, and recruiting the most eccentric residents of Dalston to help him. Documentary-style comedy, starring Jason Attar.(n)

programme.title: Anchoress (1993)
programme.desc: A peasant girl develops a fascination with a statue of the Virgin Mary, which prompts a priest to sentence her to be walled up in the church. When she discovers her mother is to be tried as a witch, she resolves to break free from her imprisonment, even if it costs her her life. Medieval drama, starring Natalie Morse, Toyah Willcox, Pete Postlethwaite and Christopher Eccleston.(n)

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: Dallas Campbell examines films from the Horizon archive to discover what science can teach people about dogs. He investigates the origins of domestic breeds and learns about the role humans played in shaping canine evolution, before asking why the bond between mankind and dogs is particularly strong - and whether it can be improved in the future.(n)

programme.desc: Peter Williams travels to the Wiltshire village home of Jim Stoodley, who at the age of 14 stole a US aircraft and took to the air with the intention of killing Adolf Hitler - earning himself a place in the Guinness Book of Records as the youngest pilot in history.(n)

programme.title: Underworld at War
programme.desc: Peter Williams investigates the ration-book fraudsters and coupon fiddlers spawned by the restrictions of the Second World War, and looks at the career of gangster Frankie Fraser, for whom the war provided an opportunity to get rich quick.(n)

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: Dallas Campbell examines films from the Horizon archive to discover what science can teach people about dogs. He investigates the origins of domestic breeds and learns about the role humans played in shaping canine evolution, before asking why the bond between mankind and dogs is particularly strong - and whether it can be improved in the future.(n)

programme.title: A Chorus of Disapproval (1988)
programme.desc: A shy widower gets entangled in romantic intrigue while rehearsing with an amateur theatrical troupe in Scarborough. Comedy based on Alan Ayckbourn's stage play, starring Jeremy Irons, Anthony Hopkins, Prunella Scales, Lionel Jeffries, Jenny Seagrove, Gareth Hunt and Patsy Kensit.(n)

programme.title: Elenya (1992)
programme.desc: Aa 12-year-old half-Italian Welsh schoolgirl stumbles across a wounded German airman during the Second World War and secretly cares for him - only for tragedy to strike as the local townsfolk discover what she has been up to. Drama, starring Pascale Delafourge Jones, Klaus Behrendt, Margaret John and Sue Jones Davies.(n)

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tour exploring and revealing Shadwell and Grove Park for locals and tourists. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay.(n)

programme.title: Underworld at War
programme.desc: Peter Williams visits Ashford and the Medway towns of Kent to recount grisly tales from the Second World War about bodies found in the rubble of the Blitz which turned out to be victims of murder.(n)

programme.title: Underworld at War
programme.desc: Peter Williams looks at the role - and the unusual odd jobs - of country policemen in the South and South East during the Second World War, when rumours of chicken stealing and illegal pig roasts were fairly common diversions from the privations of rationing.(n)

programme.title: Faking It
programme.desc: Individuals take up the challenge to reinvent themselves completely.(n)

programme.title: Henry VIII: The Mind of a Tyrant
programme.desc: David Starkey examines how the Tudor king went from being a handsome young prince whose ascension to the throne in 1509 filled the nation with hope to a ruthless monarch.(n)

programme.title: Fox
programme.desc: Drama following the lives of a London gangster and his family. Billy celebrates his birthday. Starring Peter Vaughan, Ray Winstone and Bernard Hill. Originally broadcast in 1980.(n)

programme.title: Erskineville Kings (1999)
programme.desc: A man who fled his home town to escape his abusive father decides to return once he receives word that the old man is dead. However, he faces a bitter reunion with the brother, who stayed behind to care for their father in his last days, and his ex-girlfriend, whom he abandoned with no explanation. Drama, starring Marty Denniss, Hugh Jackman and Joel Edgerton.(n)

programme.title: Sex Ed (2014)
programme.desc: A teacher arrives at a new school, and is appalled to discover his teenage students are receiving no sex education. He takes it upon himself to provide the facts of life, despite objection from the local preacher. However, his plan is complicated by his still being a virgin and falling in love with a pupil's older sister. Comedy, starring Haley Joel Osment and Lorenza Izzo.(n)
